clean_body: Media’s bias against GOP on full display during midterms. Friend, The media's bias against Republicans is on full display this election. Why is biased media so dangerous? Here's an example. Four years ago, TV's midterm coverage hammered Republican candidates and then-President Trump with 88% negative spin while sparing Democrats similarly bad press. This year, Democrats are in charge of the White House and both chambers of Congress. However, a new Media Research Center study of ABC, CBS, and NBC evening newscasts finds that Republicans still receive 87% negative news coverage. All the while, Democrats -- including the President -- are drawing far less scrutiny than the party out of power. Let me introduce myself. My name is Brent Bozell, and I'm the Founder and President of the Media Research Center (MRC). MRC is the only conservative organization dedicated to exposing the radical left-wing agenda in the media and holding them accountable for their corruption and lies. Friend, I've set a goal to raise $25,000 to STOP media bias and corruption. This year, the Big Three evening newscasts aired 115 stories that mentioned or discussed the midterm elections, with a total airtime of 213 minutes, or about 60 percent more than the 130 minutes we tallied four years ago. Here's what we found: Burying Biden Four years ago, President Trump accounted for 48% of all midterm campaign airtime in 2018, more than all of the individual House, Senate, and gubernatorial candidates combined. But the networks aren't interested in targeting President Biden this year. Biden accounted for just 34 minutes of midterm coverage , just 16% of total campaign coverage. Blasting Republicans This fall, Democrats, including President Biden, drew 13 positive comments vs. 26 negative ones for a 67% negative spin. That's not great, but not nearly as bad as Republicans. They received 94 negative comments vs. 14 positive statements, a spin score of 87% negative -- and virtually identical to the 88% negative spin for the whole GOP (including Trump) in 2018. Showcasing Democrats ' Agenda While voters consistently ranked the economy and inflation as the most important issues this fall, the broadcast networks' campaign coverage instead emphasized the agenda favored by Democratic consultants. Thus, the economy was only the fifth-most cited issue within these midterm stories, with 15 minutes of airtime. That's barely half of the time spent on anonymous and unverified allegations that GOP candidate Herschel Walker paid for two women's abortions years ago, charges Walker has vehemently denied. As the Senate majority lingers on in the Georgia run-off election, we can't let the media steal the race from voters. Here's the breakdown of the Left's coverage of issues during the midterm coverage: When the MRC was founded in 1987, over 75% of Americans trusted the left-wing media. Since then, MRC has successfully SHATTERED America 's trust in the left-wing media -- over 90% of Americans state they DO NOT trust the media anymore. But we can't do this alone, Friend. Can I count on you to join the fight for the truth? Click below to donate. Thank you, Brent Bozell Founder and President of MRC The mission of the Media Research Center is to create a media culture in America where truth and liberty flourish. The MRC is a research and education organization operating under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code, and contributions to the MRC are tax-deductible.

name: =?utf-8?Q?Caleb=20Heimlich?=
subject: The election isn't over yet!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Washington State Republican Party
body:
Help us secure Republican victories in tight races across our state!

http://wsrp.org
Ballots Are Out, Vote By November 2nd!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WeGlgXqOx-Y

With ballots out, now is the perfect time to get out there and volunteer on a conservative campaign. Every door you knock on in these local races matter a lot and the results of this election year will be used as a bellwether for the 2022 midterms.

We are also hosting another WSRP Statewide Day Of Action on Saturday, October 30th! Bring a friend or neighbor to volunteer for a few hours to help local conservatives win in November! Sign up here to join our Statewide Day Of Action! (https://form.jotform.com/212737084845058)
State Sen. Gildon On Vaccine Mandates
https://soundcloud.com/user-450836972/wagop-report-state-sen-chris-gildon-on-vaccine-mandates-and-democrat-anti-policing-laws
State Senator Chris Gildon (R - Puyallup) joins the podcast this week to discuss vaccine mandates, anti-policing laws, the Long-Term Care Tax, and how he got his start in politics. WAGOP is now on Apple Podcasts and Spotify, follow us and never miss an episode! Listen here. (https://soundcloud.com/user-450836972/wagop-report-state-sen-chris-gildon-on-vaccine-mandates-and-democrat-anti-policing-laws)
Republican Leaders Fight Inslee's Mandate
Thanks to our Republican leaders in the State House and Senate for fighting back and offering a strong rebuke of Inslee's harmful vaccine mandate. Below is an excerpt of their most recent statement to the press:

“We are already experiencing higher prices and empty shelves partially as a result of COVID's shutdowns and workforce shortages. Today's deadline, with its unnecessarily hardline consequences, will only make that worse when hundreds, if not thousands, lose their jobs and are denied unemployment benefits. It also threatens public safety by reducing our police forces at a time when crime is already rising. People feel unsafe. They are afraid of what criminals will do when there are even fewer law enforcement officers to respond. And they are worried about people's chances of survival if there aren't enough first responders to help in an emergency. The governor underestimates the potential impact of his actions.

“Coercion, intimidation, threats and public shaming are not tactics a leader should be using against the people. The governor's obvious disdain for those who are choosing to lose their jobs rather than compromise their right to make their own medical decisions is unhelpful. He is dividing the public – segregating us and turning people against each other. Yesterday, even unvaccinated health-care workers were heroes. Today, they become villains in the governor's narrative."

Read the full statement. (https://jtwilcox.houserepublicans.wa.gov/2021/10/18/republican-leaders-todays-vaccine-deadline-threatens-critical-services-public-safety/?fbclid=IwAR3WxI9H29hbbmfbZhdkDS9QjqdVd2TIRCdjNjUkUlLdeThtbUjK_WBWJuw)
Smiley Campaign Shows Grassroots Support In Latest Fundraising Quarter
Tiffany Smiley is fighting hard to retire Patty Murray and her latest fundraising numbers show not only has Tiffany raised more than $1.6 million but she also has unequivocal grassroots support, with over 10,000 donors from Washington State. Flipping a U.S. Senate seat is hard work but Tiffany has shown in the only 5 months since starting her campaign, that she is a force to be reckoned with. Learn more about how Tiffany stands on the issues here (https://www.smileyforwashington.com/issues) .
Contact:
Steve Johnson
Communications Director
Washington State Republican Party
(425) 460-0570
steve@wsrp.org (mailto:steve@wsrp.org)

| | The run-off election in Georgia is just three weeks away – 21 days to be exact. We have to do everything we can to push Senator Raphael Warnock over the top. We can’t afford to let Trump ally Herschel Walker into the Senate. **Here’s our secret weapon: Writing letters.
